Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
James Morando, Esq. of Farella Braun + Martel LLP, T:415.954.4400, for plaintiff/moving party;
James M. Gilbert, Esq. of Steckbauer Weinhart LLP, T:213.229.2868, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

PLAINTIFF HUNTER HANNOCK and COLLEEN HANCOCK's Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. The Hancocks are entitled to a right to attach order and writ of attachment in the amount of $265,012.90 on the condition that they file an undertaking in the amount of $10,000. The money in defendant Clever Machine, LLC's bank account is and is likely to remain the only asset Clever Machine has to satisfy the arbitration award in favor of the Hancocks. The liquidity of this asset, the unilateral power of Clever Machine's CEO to dispose of the asset, and Clever Machine's rejection of the Hancocks' request to pay the amount of the award issued on March 6, 2018 are sufficient to show that there is a danger that the asset will not be available and the arbitration award will be rendered ineffectual absent the grant of provisional relief. Granting provisional relief will not invade the arbitrator's province because the arbitrator has already issued his award on the Hancocks' contractual claim for $196, 831.49 as well as provided a method by which to calculate an award of prejudgment interest. The amount of the attachment is calculated as follows: $196, 831.49 for the arbitration award on the contract claim; $ 45, 751.92 for estimated prejudgment interest; and $22, 429.49 for JAMS arbitration fees. All other amounts sought to be included in the attachment are neither fixed nor readily ascertainable at this point.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
